name: Barley Egg Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Rice wine OR white wine vinegar
1 package Prepared egg roll wrappers (16 oz.)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, bring 4 cups water and 1/2 teaspoon salt to boiling.
2. Add barley; reduce heat, cover and simmer 45 minutes until barley is tender and water is absorbed.
3. In a skillet, cook ginger in oil for one minute.
4. Add scallions and red pepper. Cook, stirring until vegetables are crisp-tender.
5. Add shredded bok choy; cook for one minute until wilted. Remove from heat.
6. Stir in rice vinegar, 1 teaspoon salt and barley.
7. For each roll, brush egg roll wrapper with 1 teaspoon water.
8. Place scant 1/2 cup barley mixture horizontally across the center of the wrapper, leaving a 2-inch border on all sides.
9. Fold corner tightly over fillings, then overlap right and left corners.
10. Roll up tightly to enclose filling. Repeat with remaining barley mixture and wrappers.
11. In a deep skillet, heat oil to 375 degrees F.
12. Fry rolls in batches until golden and crisp, turning once. Drain on paper towels.
13. Serve with soy sauce, if desired. Makes 12 egg rolls.
Per Roll: 160 Calories, 4 g Protein, 7g Fat, 22g Carbohydrates, 272mg Sodium, 12 mg cholesterol.
